faqs.question: How do I get started buying a home?
faqs.answer: The first step is getting pre-approved for a mortgage — it shows sellers you're serious and tells you exactly what you can afford. We can connect you with trusted local lenders who close on time. Once you have a pre-approval letter, we schedule a free consultation to learn your goals and start the search. Most buyers go from first conversation to closed home in 60–90 days.

faqs.question: Do I need a buyer's agent? Is it free?
faqs.answer: You don't legally need one, but it costs you nothing and gets you expert representation. In Virginia, the seller typically pays both agents' commissions — so our services are free to buyers in most transactions. We handle negotiations, paperwork, deadlines, and every step from search to closing.

faqs.question: How long does it take to buy a home?
faqs.answer: From pre-approval to closing typically takes 60–90 days, though we've done it faster when needed. In a competitive market, you want to be ready to move quickly. Once an offer is accepted, closing is usually 30–45 days. We work with lenders who close on time.

faqs.question: How much do I need for a down payment?
faqs.answer: It depends on your loan type. Conventional loans typically require 3–20% down. FHA loans allow as little as 3.5% down with a 580+ credit score. VA loans (for eligible military and veterans) require $0 down — one of the best benefits available. USDA loans also allow $0 down for qualifying rural areas. We'll connect you with a lender who'll walk you through all your options.

faqs.question: What are closing costs?
faqs.answer: Buyers typically pay 2–4% of the purchase price in closing costs, which includes lender fees, title insurance, inspections, and prepaid items like homeowner's insurance and property taxes. On a $450,000 home, expect $9,000–$18,000. We can often negotiate seller concessions to help offset these costs.

faqs.question: Should I get a home inspection?
faqs.answer: Always. A home inspection typically costs $350–$550 and can reveal issues that could cost thousands later. We never recommend waiving an inspection. In competitive markets, we can use an inspection for information only (without requiring repairs) to strengthen your offer while still protecting you.

faqs.question: How much is my home worth?
faqs.answer: We offer free, no-obligation home valuations based on recent comparable sales, current market conditions, and your home's specific features. Online estimates like Zillow's Zestimate are often inaccurate by 5–15% — a real comparative market analysis from a local agent is far more reliable. Call us or fill out a contact form and we'll have a number to you within 24 hours.

faqs.question: How long will it take to sell my home?
faqs.answer: In the current Fredericksburg market, well-priced homes are averaging 28 days on market. Homes priced right and prepared well often sell in the first 7–14 days, sometimes with multiple offers. Overpriced homes can sit for months. Correct pricing from day one is the single most important factor.

faqs.question: What is your commission?
faqs.answer: Our seller's agent fee is typically 2.5–3% of the sale price. Total commission depends on what you offer the buyer's agent. We'll walk you through all costs during your free valuation appointment so there are no surprises. Our goal is to net you the most money possible, not just get an offer.

faqs.question: What should I do to prepare my home for sale?
faqs.answer: Declutter, deep clean, and handle any obvious deferred maintenance — leaky faucets, sticking doors, burnt-out bulbs. Professional photography is included in our marketing package. We'll walk through your home before listing and give you a prioritized prep list. Most of what sells a home is presentation and price.

faqs.question: Is now a good time to buy?
faqs.answer: It depends on your situation more than the market. Rates around 6.9% are higher than 2020-2021 lows, but the Fredericksburg market continues to appreciate — prices are up about 4% year over year. Waiting for rates to drop means competing with more buyers when they do. If you can afford the payment today and plan to stay 3+ years, most of our clients find it makes sense to buy now.

faqs.question: What are current mortgage rates?
faqs.answer: As of mid-2025, 30-year fixed rates are around 6.9% and 15-year rates are near 6.3%. Rates change daily and your actual rate depends on your credit score, loan type, and down payment. We can connect you with trusted local lenders who'll give you a real quote within 24 hours.

faqs.question: What is the Fredericksburg market like right now?
faqs.answer: It's still a seller's market. We have about 1.8 months of inventory — anything under 3 months favors sellers. Median sale price in the metro is around $465,000, up 4.2% from last year. Well-priced homes often receive multiple offers within the first week. Buyers need to be pre-approved and ready to move.

faqs.question: We're PCSing to the area. Where do most military families live?
faqs.answer: Most families assigned to Quantico or the Dahlgren corridor land in Stafford County. North Stafford and Aquia Harbour are closest to Quantico (5–10 minutes). Embrey Mill and Stafford Lakes are popular for their amenities and school quality. King George County is ideal for Dahlgren. We have a dedicated relocation specialist, Ray Solis, who handles military moves regularly and understands the PCS timeline.

faqs.question: How does a VA loan work?
faqs.answer: A VA loan is a zero-down mortgage available to eligible veterans, active duty service members, and surviving spouses. There's no private mortgage insurance (PMI), competitive interest rates, and flexible credit requirements. The main cost is a one-time VA funding fee, which can be rolled into the loan. It's one of the best financial benefits available to military families. We work with VA-specialized lenders who will walk you through the full process.

faqs.question: Can I buy a home if I'm only stationed here for 2-3 years?
faqs.answer: Many military buyers do. With the current appreciation rate, a 2–3 year hold often builds enough equity to sell or rent the home profitably when you PCS again. Rental demand in Stafford is very strong — especially near Quantico. We'll help you evaluate whether buying vs. renting makes sense for your specific orders and timeline.

faqs.question: Is the Fredericksburg area good for investment properties?
faqs.answer: Yes. The I-95 corridor has steady rental demand from government contractors, military families, and DC commuters priced out of Northern Virginia. Average rent for a 3-bedroom single family in Stafford or Spotsylvania is $2,200–$2,800/month. Lake Anna is strong for short-term rentals (Airbnb), with some properties grossing $70,000–$100,000+ per year.

faqs.question: What kind of return can I expect on a rental property?
faqs.answer: Long-term rentals in Stafford or Spotsylvania typically generate cap rates of 4–6%. Cash-on-cash returns depend heavily on your down payment and financing. Short-term rentals at Lake Anna can significantly exceed those numbers for waterfront properties. We'll run the numbers with you on any property you're considering.

faqs.question: What's the best neighborhood for families?
faqs.answer: Embrey Mill in Stafford and Celebrate Virginia in Spotsylvania are consistently the top picks for families. Both have strong schools, resort-style amenities, walking trails, and a real community feel. Embrey Mill has a town center you can walk to; Celebrate Virginia has larger lots. Both are in the $420,000–$650,000 range.

faqs.question: What's the best area for commuting to DC?
faqs.answer: It depends on how you commute. For VRE train commuters, Leeland Station in Stafford or Brooke Road VRE stops are ideal — you skip I-95 entirely and arrive at L'Enfant Plaza in about 75 minutes. For drivers, North Stafford gives you the shortest I-95 distance. For pure metro access, Fredericksburg Amtrak connects to Union Station in about an hour.

faqs.question: What's a good area for a first-time homebuyer?
faqs.answer: North Stafford and Stafford Lakes offer the best value for first-time buyers in our market — homes starting in the high $200s to low $400s, good schools, and strong resale. Rappahannock Landing in Spotsylvania has newer construction in the low $400s. Downtown Fredericksburg has charming older homes starting around $300,000 for buyers who want a walkable lifestyle.

faqs.question: Tell me about Lake Anna as a place to buy.
faqs.answer: Lake Anna is Virginia's most popular inland lake — 17,000 acres, excellent for boating, fishing, and watersports. It's about 35–40 minutes from Fredericksburg and 90 minutes from DC. There are two sides: the private residential side where lakefront homes have deeded water access, and the public side which is more recreational. It's a strong short-term rental market — well-positioned waterfront homes regularly generate $70,000–$100,000+ per year on Airbnb. We have a specialist who knows every cove on that lake.
